summary: Matched the Figure Get stage ground unit by implementing previously nonmatching grFigureGet functions, tightening its ground variable/data typing, updating section/symbol metadata, and flipping melee/gr/grfigureget.c from NonMatching to Matching. The decomp.dev report credited 5 new matches: grFigureGet_80219898, grFigureGet_802196F0, grFigureGet_80219B10, .rodata, and .sdata2, for +1336 matched code bytes and +48 matched data bytes.
what_changed: Implemented grFigureGet_802196F0, replacing a nonmatching marker with stage initialization logic that starts ground animation, clears ground callbacks, initializes toy/display state, selects three figure IDs, zeros per-run arrays, seeds the spawn timer from grFigureGet_804D6AC0, and registers mpJointSetCb1 with grFigureGet_80219B10.

Implemented grFigureGet_80219898, replacing a nonmatching marker with per-frame Figure Get logic: clears fighter joint_id_skip fields, spawns coin/items at randomized x positions within configured bounds while under the configured count, tracks spawned item GObjs, handles completion by updating gm registry/state flags, and calls lb_800115F4.

Adjusted grFigureGet_80219B10 enough to reach 100% match, including use of an ECBFlagStruct temporary and comparison against item classifier value 0x6, plus replacing hardcoded +/-4.5f checks with grFigureGet_804DBB60 and grFigureGet_804DBB64 extern floats.
Added a local grFigureGet_Data struct for data at grFigureGet_804D6AC0, with fields x0/x4/x8 and float bounds/offset fields xC/x10/x14 used by the matching code.
Added static Vec3 const grFigureGet_803B8470 = { 0.0f, 0.0f, 0.0f }, changing the rodata size expectation from 0x10 to 0xC.
Refined struct grFigureGet_GroundVars in src/melee/gr/types.h: x0/x4 became s32, x10 became int x10[3], and x34 became Item_GObj* x34[3], matching observed Figure Get state layout.
Updated includes in grfigureget.c for dependencies used by the matched code, including gr/stage.h, ft/ftlib.h, gm/gmregclear.h, lb/lb_00B0.h, lb/lb_00F9.h, mp/mplib.h, ty/tydisplay.h, and ty/toy.h.
Changed configure.py so melee/gr/grfigureget.c is built as Object(Matching, ...) instead of Object(NonMatching, ...).

Updated GALE01 symbols: grFigureGet_OnTouchLine and grFigureGet_OnCheckShadowRender became local-scope functions; several sdata2 floats became local @ labels; a .data label ...data.0 was added at 0x803E7D00.
Updated GALE01 splits: grfigureget.c no longer owns an .sbss range, its .sdata2 range shrank to end at 0x804DBB60, and grpushon.c .sbss was extended to 0x804D6AC8.
smart_moves: Promoted grfigureget.c to Matching only after pairing source implementation changes with config/symbol/split corrections, avoiding a source-only match that would leave data sections misattributed.
Used an explicit local data struct for grFigureGet_804D6AC0 instead of leaving it as UNK_T, which made timer/count/range fields readable and enabled consistent loads in the matched functions.

Converted grFigureGet_GroundVars fields into small fixed arrays where the code iterates over exactly three entries, matching the Figure Get stage behavior and reducing ambiguous scalar placeholder fields.
Replaced literal coordinate thresholds in grFigureGet_80219B10 with extern sdata2 float symbols, aligning the code with the binary's constant placement.
Made anonymous/local constants local in symbols.txt, including @215 through @264 for sdata2 constants, which helped match data without over-naming compiler-local constants.
Recognized that grFigureGet_803B8470 is a three-float Vec3 zero constant, correcting the rodata size from 0x10 to 0xC.
decomp_lessons: When a stage file becomes matching, audit neighboring split boundaries; here grfigureget.c losing .sbss ownership required moving the 0x804D6AC0-0x804D6AC8 area into grpushon.c's .sbss range.
Ground stage variables often need array reconstruction from repeated three-entry loops; replacing scalar placeholders with int[3], HSD_GObj*[3], and Item_GObj*[3] can be the key to matching.
Do not force meaningful global names onto compiler-local float constants; local @ labels in symbols.txt can be the correct representation for sdata2 constants.
A small near-match function may require preserving compiler-shaped expressions, such as introducing an ECBFlagStruct* temporary and comparing classifier to its numeric value, to get the final bytes.

Matching stage gameplay logic can require broad cross-system includes because ground code touches fighters, items, map collision callbacks, stage info flags, toy display, registry clear, and low-level library helpers.
Data matching should be verified alongside code matching; the bot reported code and data matches separately, with .rodata and .sdata2 becoming 100% for grfigureget.
naming_conventions: Stage functions retain the grFigureGet_ prefix with address-suffixed unknown names where semantic names are not yet established, such as grFigureGet_802196F0 and grFigureGet_80219898.
Stage lifecycle/callback functions use named On* conventions where known: grFigureGet_OnDemoInit, grFigureGet_OnInit, grFigureGet_OnLoad, grFigureGet_OnTouchLine, grFigureGet_OnCheckShadowRender.
Per-stage ground variable structs follow gr<Stage>_GroundVars naming, here struct grFigureGet_GroundVars.
New local data struct followed the file/stage prefix convention: grFigureGet_Data.
Anonymous/local data constants in symbols.txt use @NNN labels rather than globally named grFigureGet_* labels when the evidence points to local compiler constants.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(8) in grFigureGet_802196F0, indicating stack layout padding was needed for a match.
Introduced temporaries and explicit control flow for HSD_Randi paths, including zero checks before random range calls, to match generated code.
Copied a zero Vec3 constant into a local Point3d position before mutating x/y fields in grFigureGet_80219898, matching rodata use.
Preserved exact three-iteration loops for Figure Get arrays and item tracking.
Used explicit numeric constants where the diff shows they matched better than enum names, e.g. item_gobj->classifier == 0x6 in grFigureGet_80219B10.
Moved constants to sdata2 externs for final comparison code instead of using inline literals.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 improvements: matched code 65.80% (+0.03%, +1336 bytes), linked code 37.92% (+0.05%, +1896 bytes), matched data 38.69% (+48 bytes), linked data 33.00% (+208 bytes).
decomp.dev bot listed 5 new grfigureget matches: grFigureGet_80219898 (+628 bytes), grFigureGet_802196F0 (+416 bytes), .sdata2 (+19 bytes), .rodata (+16 bytes), and grFigureGet_80219B10 (+3 bytes).
decomp.dev bot noted one unmatched-item regression: main/melee/gr/grpushon .sbss went from 50.00% to 25.00%, apparently due to split ownership changes around 0x804D6AC0.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename grFigureGet_802196F0 or grFigureGet_80219898 to semantic names.
Search for additional grFigureGet_804D6AC0 struct refinements or relocation from extern to a typed global definition.
Check whether the grpushon .sbss regression noted by decomp.dev was resolved in a later split/symbol cleanup.
Compare other stage files using three-slot item/figure arrays for reusable gr*_GroundVars typing patterns.
Search for uses of grFigureGet_804DBB60 and grFigureGet_804DBB64 to determine better semantic names for the x-bound constants.
